title: Professional Summary
text:
Kshitij Gupta is an ML Engineer with approximately 6 years of total experience, including 4 years in production environments. His career progression spans three distinct phases: AI Engineering at a US startup (MirrorSize), founding engineer at a DRM startup (ttdsoft), graduate studies at IIIT Hyderabad, and production ML roles at Typeface (enterprise generative AI) and CARS24 (India's largest used-car marketplace).

His work spans computer vision, generative AI, and cloud-native MLOps. He has shipped complete end-to-end ML pipelines ranging from sub-5MB on-device TFLite models running fully offline on Android devices with zero cloud latency, to large-scale Kubernetes inference clusters on GCP and Azure serving Fortune-500 enterprise clients at production scale.

He is equally comfortable owning the full stack: problem formulation, data annotation pipeline design (CVAT), dataset versioning, model training and evaluation, inference optimisation (ONNX, TFLite, Triton), API development (FastAPI), containerisation (Docker, Kubernetes, KServe), and production deployment with CI/CD (GitHub Actions).

Notable: Dean's List at IIIT Hyderabad, KVPY Scholar (IISc), and a demonstrated preference for self-hosted, offline-first, privacy-respecting infrastructure — as seen in projects like NeuralDocs AI and this very portfolio Q&A system.

title: M.Tech — IIIT Hyderabad
text:
Master of Technology (M.Tech) in Computer Science from IIIT Hyderabad (August 2021 to May 2023). IIIT Hyderabad is consistently ranked among India's top 3 research institutes for computer science and AI.

Coursework focused on advanced and interdisciplinary topics:
- Natural Language Processing (NLP): transformer architectures, sequence modelling, attention mechanisms
- Statistical AI: probabilistic graphical models, Bayesian inference, reinforcement learning
- Distributed Systems: consensus algorithms, fault tolerance, distributed databases
- Blockchain and Cryptocurrencies: consensus protocols, smart contracts, decentralised applications
- Neural and Cognitive Modelling: biologically-inspired neural networks, cognitive architectures

Graduated on the Dean's List — awarded to the top percentile of students for academic excellence.

Relevant coursework projects involved building distributed ML pipelines, NLP-based information retrieval systems, and blockchain-based data provenance tracking.

title: B.Tech — IIIT Delhi
text:
Bachelor of Technology (B.Tech) in Computer Science from IIIT Delhi (August 2015 to May 2019). IIIT Delhi is a government-funded institute of national importance under the Act of Parliament, known for its strong industry-oriented computer science curriculum and entrepreneurship culture.

Coursework included:
- Computer Vision: image processing, feature extraction, object detection and recognition
- Machine Learning: supervised and unsupervised learning, model evaluation, bias-variance tradeoff
- GPU Computing: CUDA programming, parallel algorithms, GPU architecture optimisation
- Image Analysis: segmentation, morphological operations, texture analysis

Undergraduate projects involved building GPU-accelerated image processing pipelines and implementing ML algorithms from scratch in C++.

Notable: One of the early batches at IIIT Delhi, helping shape the institute's CS culture through student-led initiatives.

title: Technical Skills
text:
Programming Languages: Python (primary, 6+ years), C, C++ (used for DRM systems, CUDA kernels), JavaScript, TypeScript (used for full-stack and Electron apps).

AI / ML Frameworks: PyTorch (primary deep learning framework), OpenCV (image processing pipelines), Scikit-Learn (classical ML), ONNX (model interchange and optimisation), TFLite / TensorFlow Lite (on-device mobile inference), Triton Inference Server (production model serving), Hugging Face Transformers (NLP and LLM workflows).

LLM / RAG Stack: LangChain (RAG orchestration), Qdrant (vector search), ChromaDB (embedding store), RAGAS (RAG evaluation), LLM-as-judge evaluation methodologies, Sentence Transformers (embedding models), Groq (ultra-fast LLM inference), OpenAI API, Anthropic / Claude API, Ollama (local LLM hosting).

Web & Backend: FastAPI (primary web framework, async handlers, SSE streaming), Django (ORM-heavy applications), Flask (lightweight APIs and prototypes), Node.js (DRM licensing server, encryption APIs), React.js, Next.js (full-stack React with SSR), Electron.js (cross-platform desktop applications).

Cloud & Infrastructure: Docker (containerisation and multi-stage builds), Docker Compose (multi-service orchestration), Kubernetes (production cluster management), KServe (Kubernetes-native model serving), GCP (GKE, Cloud Storage, Cloud Build), Azure (AKS, VM-based inference), Proxmox (self-hosted hypervisor and homelab), GitHub Actions (CI/CD pipelines and automated deployments), systemd (Linux service management).

Databases & Storage: PostgreSQL (primary relational DB), ChromaDB (vector and embedding storage), Redis (caching, semantic cache, pub/sub), Qdrant (production vector search), FAISS (approximate nearest neighbour search).

Specialties: On-device inference optimisation (TFLite, model pruning, quantisation), model serving (Triton, KServe, custom FastAPI containers), RAG pipeline design (chunking strategies, embedding selection, hybrid search), MLOps automation (CI/CD for ML, dataset versioning, automated retraining), self-hosted and privacy-preserving AI infrastructure (offline-first deployment).

title: CARS24 — ML Engineer
text:
ML Engineer at CARS24, Gurgaon (June 2024 to June 2025). CARS24 is India's largest used-car marketplace, processing thousands of vehicle inspections daily across 200+ cities.

Key contributions:

1. End-to-end Image Validation Pipeline (flagship project):
Designed and deployed a production-grade image validation system using KServe and Triton Inference Server on GCP Kubernetes (GKE). The system validates per-part car inspection images in real-time — checking for quality, completeness, and damage markers. Reduced per-vehicle inspection time by 20 to 30 minutes by eliminating manual image review. Handles thousands of concurrent inspections with auto-scaling inference pods.

2. On-device TFLite model (<5MB) for part classification and pose detection:
Built and quantised a deep learning model under 5MB that runs fully offline on Android devices used by field inspectors. The model classifies car parts (door, bumper, hood, tyre, etc.) and estimates their spatial pose from camera input. Zero cloud round-trip latency — critical for inspectors in parking lots and service centres with poor connectivity. Used post-training quantisation (int8), model pruning, and ONNX-to-TFLite conversion to hit the size budget.

3. Underbody oil-leak detection module:
Extended the damage-estimation pipeline with a computer vision module that detects oil leaks from underbody vehicle images. Added coverage for a previously undetected damage category, improving the overall damage prediction accuracy by approximately 5%.

4. Full MLOps lifecycle ownership:
Managed the complete pipeline end to end: designed CVAT-based annotation schemas and workflows, implemented dataset versioning with DVC, orchestrated training jobs on GCP Vertex AI, and automated cloud deployment via GitHub Actions triggers. Removed manual handoffs between the annotation team, ML team, and backend engineering team — reducing model iteration cycle from weeks to days.

title: Typeface India — Software Engineer
text:
Software Engineer at Typeface India, Hyderabad (July 2023 to March 2024). Typeface is an enterprise generative AI platform for branded content creation, valued at over $1B (unicorn).

Key contributions:

1. Production generative image features for Fortune-500 clients:
Shipped generative image capabilities for LG (consumer electronics marketing), Marks & Spencer (retail and fashion campaigns), and Ford (automotive advertising). Owned the full pipeline from foundation model integration to deployed inference — including prompt engineering, output quality evaluation, and latency optimisation. These features were used in production marketing campaigns reaching millions of end-users.

2. Multi-model integration:
Integrated and productionised three major foundation models — Stable Diffusion (open-source, self-hosted on Azure), Google ImageGen (GCP Vertex AI), and DALL-E (OpenAI API). Built a unified abstraction layer that allowed switching between models based on cost, latency, and quality requirements without changing downstream pipeline code.

3. Enterprise-scale image generation pipelines:
Designed and built pipelines for: scene generation (product-in-context synthesis), inpainting (seamless object removal and replacement), outpainting (intelligent canvas expansion), colour-palette extraction (automatic brand-colour detection from reference images), and style-transfer (applying brand visual identity across image variants).

4. High-availability inference infrastructure:
Maintained inference infrastructure across Azure (AKS for self-hosted models) and GCP (Vertex AI for managed services). Implemented fallback routing, request queuing, and auto-scaling to meet enterprise SLA commitments (99.5% uptime, <2s median latency).

title: ttdsoft — Software Engineer (Founding)
text:
Founding Software Engineer at ttdsoft, New Delhi (May 2020 to June 2021). An early-stage software startup focused on digital rights management (DRM) for content creators and distributors. Joined as one of the first engineers and wore many hats: product architecture, full-stack development, infrastructure.

Key contributions:

1. Cross-platform DRM suite:
Architected and built a complete DRM ecosystem spanning three platforms: Windows (C++/Qt, kernel-level encryption hooks), macOS (Swift, FileVault integration), and Android (Kotlin, verified Android Keystore integration). Products included ePendrive (a USB drive with hardware-locked encrypted content distribution), Secure Browser (a locked-down Chromium variant that prevents unauthorised copying, printing, and screen-capture), and an encrypted video player with DRM-protected streaming and screen-capture protection.

2. Stack migration for performance:
Re-architected the core product stack from Python (PyQt) to Electron.js (TypeScript + React), which significantly improved UI performance, reduced memory footprint, and enabled seamless cross-platform builds. This migration was critical for onboarding enterprise clients who required consistent behaviour across Windows and macOS.

3. Licensing and encryption API:
Built a Node.js REST API for license generation, validation, hardware binding, and content encryption key management. The API handled activation/deactivation workflows, offline grace periods, and device limits.

4. Zero-cloud infrastructure:
Self-hosted the entire backend infrastructure on bare-metal servers in a co-location facility — databases (PostgreSQL), API servers, email OTP system, license validation services. Zero cloud spend, zero vendor dependency. This taught deep systems administration, Linux hardening, and operational resilience.

title: MirrorSize US Inc. — AI Engineer
text:
AI Engineer at MirrorSize US Inc., New Delhi (May 2019 to June 2020). MirrorSize is a US-based startup developing virtual fitting room technology for e-commerce — allowing customers to visualise how clothes will fit without trying them on physically.

Key contribution:

Built an end-to-end person-segmentation and body-measurement estimation pipeline using Mask R-CNN. The system takes a single RGB image of a person (captured via a standard smartphone camera) and performs:
- Person segmentation (isolating the person from the background)
- Body-contour extraction (identifying shoulders, waist, hips, inseam landmarks)
- Body-measurement estimation (predicting bust, waist, hip, and inseam dimensions from a single 2D image)

This technology enabled virtual fit recommendations without requiring specialist 3D scanning hardware — the core technical capability powering MirrorSize's product. The pipeline was built using PyTorch and OpenCV, with custom post-processing for measurement regression from segmentation masks.

This role was Kshitij's first professional AI/ML position, where he gained deep experience in the full computer vision pipeline: dataset collection and curation, model training and evaluation, and deployment to a production inference API.

title: LLM Eval Harness
text:
LLM Eval Harness — an automated evaluation pipeline for RAG and agentic LLM systems. Built to solve the problem of quantifying LLM output quality in production.

Stack: Python, FastAPI, ChromaDB, PostgreSQL, GitHub Actions, Sentence Transformers, HTML/CSS for reporting.

What it does: Provides a YAML-driven evaluation harness that tests LLM systems across 4 providers — Ollama (local, self-hosted), Groq (ultra-fast inference), OpenAI (GPT-4, GPT-3.5), and Anthropic (Claude).

Evaluation methodology: Implements LLM-as-judge scoring (using a separate evaluator LLM to grade responses). Metrics tracked:
- Faithfulness: Does the response stay true to the provided context?
- Relevance: How well does the response address the user's question?
- Hallucination rate: Frequency of claims not supported by the provided context
- Latency: End-to-end response time (p95, median)
- Context adherence: Percentage of claims that cite specific source chunks

RAG evaluation pipeline: Uses ChromaDB with sentence-transformers for document ingestion, chunking (recursive character text splitter), embedding, retrieval, and benchmark execution against a curated test set.

CI/CD integration: A GitHub Actions workflow runs evaluations on every pull request. If hallucination rate exceeds a configurable threshold (default: >15%), the deployment is automatically blocked — ensuring production quality gates are enforced without manual review.

Outcome: Reduced undetected hallucination regressions in production from weekly occurrences to near-zero.

title: Image Similarity Search
text:
Image Similarity Search — a production-style semantic image retrieval engine that demonstrates end-to-end vector search architecture.

Stack: Python, FastAPI, PyTorch, FAISS, Streamlit, CLIP ViT-B/32.

What it does: Uses CLIP (Contrastive Language-Image Pre-training) ViT-B/32 embeddings combined with FAISS Approximate Nearest Neighbour (ANN) indexing to retrieve semantically similar images from a dataset with sub-millisecond search latency.

Key features:
- Three index types implemented and benchmarked: HNSW (hierarchical navigable small world, best recall), IVF (inverted file index, best speed/memory tradeoff), and exact/flat (brute-force, ground truth for benchmarking)
- Comprehensive recall vs latency trade-off analysis with visual charts
- Incremental indexing — new images can be added without rebuilding the entire index
- Metadata filtering (filter by tags, date ranges, categories before similarity search)
- Embedding caching with LRU eviction for frequently accessed queries

Architecture: FastAPI backend serves search and indexing endpoints. Streamlit frontend provides an interactive UI for image uploads, top-K similarity display, similarity scoring with confidence percentages, and a real-time embedding space visualisation using PCA/t-SNE projection.

Performance: Achieves <1ms search latency at 95th percentile on a dataset of 100K images using HNSW indexing, with >98% recall at 10.

title: NeuralDocs AI
text:
NeuralDocs AI — a local-first AI document intelligence platform (also referred to as AI Doc). Designed for users who cannot or will not send sensitive documents to cloud APIs.

Stack: FastAPI, Ollama, ChromaDB, Docker, Docker Compose, Next.js, TailwindCSS.

What it does: A self-hosted PDF analyser that runs LLM inference entirely locally via Ollama — zero data egress, everything stays on your machine or server. No external API calls, no data leaves your network.

Capabilities:
- PDF parsing and text extraction (supports scanned documents via OCR fallback)
- Document chunking with overlap for context preservation
- Semantic retrieval (ChromaDB + Sentence Transformer embeddings) for finding relevant sections
- Structured Q&A: ask questions about the document and get cited answers with source page references
- Automatic summary generation (executive summary, key findings, section-by-section breakdown)
- Key insight extraction (entities, dates, decisions, action items)

Deployment: A single docker compose up on any Linux server — VPS, Proxmox homelab, bare-metal, or even a Raspberry Pi cluster. No cloud dependencies, no API keys, no subscription fees.

Use cases: Legal document review, academic paper analysis, contract due diligence, internal policy Q&A — any scenario where document confidentiality is paramount.

title: ONNXLab
text:
ONNXLab — a browser-native ONNX model visualisation and inference platform. No installation, no backend, no GPU required.

Stack: Next.js, TypeScript, React Flow, ONNX Runtime Web, WebGPU, TailwindCSS, Dagre layout engine.

What it does: An interactive platform where you can upload any ONNX model file (.onnx) and immediately:
- Visualise its computation graph: Explore the full model architecture with interactive node rendering — zoom, pan, expand/collapse subgraphs. Each node shows its type (Conv, Relu, Softmax, etc.), input/output tensor shapes, and parameter counts.
- Inspect tensor metadata: Click any node to see input/output tensor dimensions, data types (float32, int8, etc.), quantisation parameters, and memory footprint estimates.
- Run inference in-browser: Upload test data or use the built-in tensor generator (random noise, specific patterns, or preprocessed image). ONNX Runtime Web executes the model using WebGPU for GPU acceleration with automatic WASM fallback for devices without WebGPU support.
- Analyse outputs: View prediction results, top-K extraction with confidence scoring (for classification models), feature map visualisations (for convolutional layers), and custom label mapping.

Notable: Everything runs entirely in the browser. No file is uploaded to any server — the ONNX file stays local. Built for ML engineers who want to quickly inspect and test models without setting up a Python environment.

Links: GitHub available, live demo available.

title: Honours & Awards
text:
Academic and competitive achievements:

1. Dean's List — IIIT Hyderabad (M.Tech programme, 2023):
Awarded for academic excellence in the M.Tech Computer Science programme. The Dean's List recognises the top-performing students across all specialisations each semester. Kshitij received this distinction during the Spring 2023 semester for outstanding performance across NLP, distributed systems, and neural modelling coursework.

2. KVPY Scholar — 2014 (IISc Bangalore):
Kishore Vaigyanik Protsahan Yojana (KVPY) is a national scholarship programme initiated by the Department of Science and Technology, Government of India, and administered by the Indian Institute of Science (IISc), Bangalore. Awarded to students with exceptional aptitude in scientific research. One of India's most prestigious and competitive science scholarships — selection rate is approximately 1% of applicants.

3. Google Lunar X-Prize Moonbots — Top-30 Finalist (Global):
Part of the Google Lunar X-Prize competition. Moonbots was an international robotics competition sponsored by Google, where student teams from around the world designed and programmed autonomous lunar rovers to complete simulated missions. Competed against hundreds of teams globally and placed in the Top 30.

4. JSTSE Scholar — 2012 (Delhi Government):
Junior Science Talent Search Examination (JSTSE) awarded by the Department of Education, Government of Delhi. This examination identifies and nurtures students with outstanding aptitude in science and mathematics at the secondary school level. Awarded for exceptional performance in the science talent search examination.

title: Job Availability & Preferences
text:
Kshitij is currently open to new opportunities and actively looking for roles where he can apply his ML engineering and MLOps experience.

Preferred roles: ML Engineer (production ML / inference serving), MLOps Engineer (infrastructure, CI/CD for ML, model deployment), Backend Engineer (Python, API design with AI/ML components), AI Infrastructure Engineer (self-hosted and cloud inference infrastructure). Open to adjacent roles like Applied Scientist (with production engineering focus) or Developer Advocate (for ML/MLOps tooling).

Preferred engagement: Full-time or contract / freelance — open to both based on the opportunity.

Notice period: Immediate to 2 weeks. Currently not employed, available to start quickly.

Open to relocation: Open to remote roles worldwide. Willing to relocate for the right in-person or hybrid role. Based in New Delhi, India (IST, UTC+5:30).

Work authorisation: Authorised to work in India. Open to remote roles internationally as an independent contractor.

Languages spoken: English (fluent, professional working proficiency), Hindi (native).

LinkedIn: linkedin.com/in/horz7 for the complete profile and recommendations.

title: Interests & Personality
text:
Hobbies & interests:
- Gaming: Enjoys strategy and simulation games. Appreciates game design, systems thinking, and optimisation challenges that games present.
- Movies, TV shows, and anime: Watches across genres — sci-fi, thriller, psychological drama, and slice-of-life anime. Enjoys analysing storytelling, world-building, and character development.
- Open-source: Contributes to and maintains open-source projects. Believes in building tools that other developers find useful.

What excites you technically:
- On-device and edge AI inference: The challenge of making models small, fast, and reliable enough to run without a cloud connection.
- Systems programming and tooling: Building developer tools that make other engineers more productive — model visualisers, eval harnesses, CI/CD pipelines.
- MLOps infrastructure: Designing the systems that make ML teams move fast without breaking things — automated deployment, monitoring, retraining, and quality gates.
- Self-hosted and offline-first: Building for people who value privacy, control, and the ability to run software on their own infrastructure without vendor lock-in.

Working style:
- Async-first — prefers written communication for decisions, design docs, and technical discussions.
- Documentation-driven — writes things down: architecture decisions, runbooks, API specs.
- Ownership mindset — takes full responsibility for what ships: design, implementation, testing, deployment, and post-mortem.
- Prefers small, focused teams with clear goals over large, process-heavy organisations.

Fun fact: At CARS24, Kshitij optimised a TFLite model down to 4.8MB — small enough to fit on a 5MB email attachment — and deployed it to thousands of Android devices used by car inspectors across 200+ Indian cities. The model runs entirely offline, processing images in sub-100ms on budget Android phones. This kind of constraint engineering is what gets him excited to come to work.
